Implication: Trade-specific; not market-condition-based. Independent by definition.
Computation: STO legs sum / BTO legs sum from regex
tool_tip_info: Short-to-Long premium Ratio — structural quality gate for calendar/DC trades. Higher = more relative value captured by the short legs at entry. Calc: sum(STO leg premium) / sum(BTO leg premium) at entry.

Implication: Trade-specific. Correlated with VIX (higher vol -> richer premium) but measured at trade level.
tool_tip_info: Per-1-lot net premium at entry, in price units (notional/100). Negative = debit paid, positive = credit received (matches OO's db/cr labels). Calc: sum(qty × signed_leg_price) / num_contracts from OO trade log; anti-correlated r=-1.00 with margin_per_contract.

Implication: Trade-specific. Driven by wing width and underlying price, not market regime.
tool_tip_info: Margin required per 1-lot of the structure at entry, in dollars. Higher = wider spreads or more vol-sensitive structure. Calc: margin_req / num_contracts from OO trade log; anti-correlated r=-1.00 with premium_per_contract.
Short-put leg entry premium, price units (positive = credit collected). Requires per-leg OO export with Short Put premium column. Calc: short-put entry price from leg-level data.
Long-put leg entry premium, price units (negative = debit paid). Requires per-leg OO export with Long Put premium column. Calc: long-put entry price from leg-level data.
Short-call leg entry premium, price units (positive = credit collected). Requires per-leg OO export with Short Call premium column. Calc: short-call entry price from leg-level data.
Long-call leg entry premium, price units (negative = debit paid). Requires per-leg OO export with Long Call premium column. Calc: long-call entry price from leg-level data.

TB Notes: Primary: market.intraday (block's underlying ticker — SPX/QQQ/SPY/IWM/etc.). Fallback: OO trade log 'Movement' column (points) converted to pct via underlying daily open.
OO Notes: Matches OO's Movement entry filter (expressed as percentage for scale-consistency with Gap_Pct)
Implication: Same-day intraday drift at entry time. Independent signal from Gap (= overnight). Positive = underlying has rallied from today's open; negative = sold off.
Computation: Intraday lookup + OO CSV fallback (points→pct conversion)
tool_tip_info: Same-day intraday drift from today's open to the trade's entry timestamp, %. Positive = rallied from open, negative = sold off. Scale-consistent version of OO's 'Movement' entry filter. Calc: (intraday_open_at_entry − daily_open) / daily_open × 100. Requires intraday bar data; OO 'Movement' fallback.

The Dark Index (DIX) is a dollar-weighted measure of the Dark Pool Indicator (DPI) of the S&P 500 components. When the DIX is higher, market sentiment in dark pools is generally more bullish. When the DIX is lower, it is more bearish or uncertain.

Gamma Exposure (GEX) is a dollar-denominated measure of option market-makers' hedging obligations. When GEX is high, the option market is implying that volatility will be low. When GEX is low, volatility is high, and while we expect a choppy market, further losses are unlikely.
